title: Ready golf: the pace-of-play covenant
microFact: Nobody minds a beginner who plays fast. Everybody minds a scratch golfer who plays slow.
lesson:
Pace of play is the #1 unwritten contract at every club. Your group's job: keep up with the group AHEAD of you — not just stay ahead of the group behind.

READY GOLF means whoever is ready plays (safely), instead of strictly honoring order. It's now officially encouraged in stroke play. Practical habits:

• Think about your club and shot WHILE others play, not after
• Walk briskly to YOUR ball, not in a chatty cluster
• Limit searches — three minutes is the rule limit
• Take one practice swing, not four
• On the green, line up your putt while others putt
• Leave your bag/cart on the exit side of the green toward the next tee

If your group falls a clear hole behind, wave the group behind through: stand aside, signal them to play. Doing this unprompted earns more respect than a birdie.

quiz.question: Your group has fallen a full hole behind the group ahead. What does etiquette expect?
options: Invite the group behind to play through
Skip a hole to catch up
Nothing, as long as nobody complains
Have your best player hit first from then on
quiz.explanation: Falling a hole behind with a group waiting = wave them through. It's the classic courtesy everyone notices.
